name: lookup_always_none
code:
proof fn phi_1_lookup_always_none(h: Hashtable, k: AbstractKey)
    requires
        h.dom().contains(k),
    ensures
        hashtable_lookup(h, k) is None,
{
}
reason: If a present key always maps to None, all get-request replies would incorrectly report missing values

name: retransmit_sends_nothing
code:
proof fn phi_2_retransmit_sends_nothing(pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState, out: Set<Packet>)
    requires
        pre.delegation_map.is_complete(),
        spontaneously_retransmit(pre, post, out),
    ensures
        out == Set::<Packet>::empty(),
{
}
reason: If retransmit never produces packets, unacknowledged messages are silently lost and reliability is broken

name: set_request_no_h_change
code:
proof fn phi_3_set_request_no_h_change(pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState, pkt: Packet, out: Set<Packet>)
    requires
        pkt.msg is Message,
        pre.delegation_map.is_complete(),
        next_set_request(pre, post, pkt, out),
    ensures
        post.h == pre.h,
{
}
reason: If set requests never modify the hashtable, the key-value store cannot be written to at all

name: bulk_update_excludes_new_keys
code:
proof fn phi_4_bulk_update_excludes_new_keys(h: Hashtable, kr: KeyRange<AbstractKey>, u: Hashtable, k: AbstractKey)
    requires
        u.dom().contains(k),
        kr.contains(k),
        !h.dom().contains(k),
    ensures
        !bulk_update_domain(h, kr, u).contains(k),
{
}
reason: If bulk_update_domain drops keys present in the update table within the range, delegate operations silently lose data

name: send_always_suppressed
code:
proof fn phi_5_send_always_suppressed(pre: SingleDelivery<Message>, post: SingleDelivery<Message>, m: Message, dst: AbstractEndPoint, sm: Option<SingleMessage<Message>>, params: AbstractParameters)
    requires
        SingleDelivery::<Message>::send_single_message(pre, post, m, dst, sm, params),
    ensures
        sm is None,
{
}
reason: If send_single_message always returns None, no replies or delegates are ever transmitted, making the protocol non-functional
